content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch diff against origin/master and read the touched implementation areas. cargo test --lib, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, and cargo fmt --check passed locally. I found two fix-now tracing issues: the new reconciliation summary counters are not reliable on replay/verification paths, and the br adapter spans now log full argv payloads into structured tracing. nix build was still running when I stopped waiting, so I did not verify a final nix result.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: reconcile_success emits incorrect summary counts because it diffs persisted state instead of using the actual reconciliation results. In src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:203-207 and 386-396, beads_created and planned_elsewhere_mappings are derived from before/after journal snapshots via helpers at 418-476. That miscounts common recovery paths: src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3343-3351 appends ProposedBeadCreated even when replay is only recovering an already-created bead, and src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1018-1045 rewrites planned-elsewhere mappings in place during verification, so the summary can over-report created beads and report 0 planned-elsewhere mappings even when verification just succeeded. affected_files: ["src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs", "src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs"]. remediation_hint: return explicit counters from reconcile_proposed_beads_after_success and verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success and log those, rather than inferring counts from durable-state deltas.
   Rationale: The new reconciliation summary is supposed to tell operators what this pass actually did. Under replay and verification flows it becomes misleading exactly when someone is debugging retries, which defeats the purpose of the added tracing.
2. finding_summary: the new low-level br spans log full command lines, including free-form payload arguments, into the operation field. src/adapters/br_process.rs:321-323 turns every argv element into display_string(), and src/adapters/br_process.rs:655-748 records that string on exec_read/exec_mutation/exec_json spans. create/comment commands can carry long descriptions and review text (see src/adapters/br_process.rs:404-418 and create_bead's use of description arguments), so successful traces now emit unbounded user-authored content into logs instead of a bounded operation name. affected_files: ["src/adapters/br_process.rs"]. remediation_hint: keep operation to a bounded command/subcommand identifier and, if needed, log selected arguments in separate redacted fields rather than serializing the whole argv.
   Rationale: This is an observability safety issue, not just verbosity. It can leak full proposal/comment bodies and produce multi-line high-cardinality fields that make downstream log aggregation and search unreliable.
